evidenceLog.note: Day 46. Ceasefire expires Apr 22 (6 days). Gap between in-principle and formal is significant. US may use ambiguity as leverage while blockade tightens. Cross-ref ev_139. talksTrack: supports direct_talks_resume (0.30).

evidenceLog.text: Treasury waiver authorizing certain Iranian crude purchases expires this weekend and will NOT be renewed. Tightens economic blockade beyond naval interdiction. 230 loaded oil tankers remain trapped in Gulf. ECB and IMF issuing stagflation warnings tied to supply disruptions. North Sea oil at record highs.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. H3/H4-directional: waiver expiry removes last legal channel for Iranian oil exports, deepening economic isolation. Adds resolution prerequisite (sanctions policy reversal). No indicator fired.
evidenceLog.note: Day 46. Source trust 0.50 (unknown in source_db). Waiver expiry verifiable via Treasury.gov. 230 loaded tankers refines stranded_vessels datafeed.

evidenceLog.text: USS Spruance (DDG-111) redirected Iranian-flagged cargo vessel attempting to evade US blockade after leaving Bandar Abbas. Vessel heading back to Iran. Blockade enforcement actively operational with physical interdictions in Gulf of Oman.
evidenceLog.note: Matches watchpoint: US naval blockade implementation - does Navy actually intercept ships?. Domain keywords: blockade, iran, iranian. No pre-registered indicator match — review whether this warrants a new indicator or evidence log entry. Source trust: 50% (moderate).

evidenceLog.text: GOVERNANCE WARNING on bayesian_update: stale_evidence. Reason: Evidence ev_144: USS Spruance (DDG-111) redirected Iranian-flagged cargo vessel attempting to eva

Pakistani army chief Gen Asim Munir arrived Tehran Apr 15 to push for round 2 US-Iran talks before ceasefire expires Apr 22. Three sticking points from Islamabad round 1: Iran nuclear enrichment (US demands 20-year moratorium), Strait of Hormuz reopening terms, and war damage compensation. Vance expected to lead US delegation for round 2. Framework deal being discussed per Axios.
evidenceLog.note: Matches indicator ceasefire (anti indicators). (already fired). Source trust: 50% (moderate).

evidenceLog.text: GOVERNANCE WARNING on bayesian_update: stale_evidence. Reason: Evidence ev_145: Pakistani army chief Gen Asim Munir arrived Tehran Apr 15 to push for round 2 US

White House optimistic about Iran deal as key Pakistani negotiator visits Tehran. Pakistan attempting to arrange second round of US-Iran peace talks after Islamabad marathon session ended without agreement.
evidenceLog.note: Matches watchpoint: Mine-clearing progress - USS Peterson + Murphy + UUVs. Domain keywords: iran, islamabad, talks. No pre-registered indicator match — review whether this warrants a new indicator or evidence log entry. Source trust: 72% (moderate).

evidenceLog.text: CENTCOM declares US naval blockade of Iranian ports fully implemented. Three carrier groups (Lincoln, GHW Bush, Gerald R Ford), 10 destroyers, 2 amphibious assault ships enforcing. Only 11 commercial ships transited Strait Apr 15 — 90% below pre-war normal of ~40/day. Blockade estimated to cost Iran USD 435M/day.
evidenceLog.note: Official CENTCOM statement confirmed by CNBC, USNI News, The National. Force disposition verified across multiple outlets. Traffic data from ship-tracking services.

evidenceLog.text: GOVERNANCE WARNING on bayesian_update: stale_evidence. Reason: Evidence ev_147: CENTCOM declares US naval blockade of Iranian ports fully implemented. Three car

IRGC Maj Gen Ali Abdollahi warns US naval blockade constitutes prelude to ceasefire violation. Iran threatens to block ALL exports/imports in Persian Gulf, Sea of Oman, and Red Sea if US blockade persists. Hardline parliament members publicly oppose concessions.
evidenceLog.note: Direct IRGC statement reported by Al Jazeera. Threat to expand blockade to Red Sea is new escalation vector. Ceasefire expires Apr 21 — 5 days away.

evidenceLog.text: Pakistani military and interior officials visit Tehran Apr 16 to facilitate second-round US-Iran negotiations. White House says good about prospects of deal. VP Vance expected to lead delegation if talks materialize. Ceasefire expires Apr 21. Meanwhile, Malta-flagged VLCC Agios Fanourios I became first crude carrier to transit westward through Hormuz since blockade (headed to Basrah, Iraq). Sanctioned Chinese tanker Rich Starry also transited Apr 14.
evidenceLog.note: Pakistan mediation from CNBC/Al Jazeera. Ship transits from Newsweek/ship-tracking data. Diplomatic optimism is WHITE HOUSE rhetoric — not backed by concrete concessions. Iran demands: full war cessation, sanctions lifted, nuclear enrichment protected. These are maximalist positions.

evidenceLog.text: Macron and Starmer hold international summit Apr 17 on reopening Strait of Hormuz. Dozens of countries participated (US not invited). Coalition planning mine-clearing and maritime warning system rather than warship escorts. France and Britain leading military planning echoing Ukraine coalition-of-the-willing model.

DefSec Hegseth: US blockade of Iranian ports to last "as long as it takes." USS George H.W. Bush carrier group ordered to Middle East (6,000 additional troops). US military expanding blockade scope — will target Iran-linked ships worldwide, not just Hormuz. Over 10,000 troops and 100+ aircraft currently enforcing. Hormuz traffic less than 10% of pre-war average. 13 ships turned back since blockade began Apr 13. JCS Chair Caine clarified blockade targets Iranian ports/coastline, not strait itself.
US naval blockade of Iranian ports fully implemented; Pentagon confirms 13 ships turned back in 24 hours; blockade halts all economic trade in/out of Iran by sea

US announces full blockade of Iranian ports, cutting off ~90% of Iran seaborne trade. Blockade declared after Iran failed to reopen Strait of Hormuz per ceasefire terms. Iran charging ships for passage and requiring coordination with armed forces.

Iranian FM Araghchi declares Strait of Hormuz completely open to commercial vessels for duration of 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire. Ships must use coordinated route. Trump confirms, claims Iran agreed to never close Hormuz again. Oil prices drop 12%. Bloomberg reports few tankers observed exiting before announcement — actual traffic resumption unconfirmed.

US blockade of Iranian ports remains in full force despite Hormuz reopening declaration. CENTCOM: blockade enforced on all ships entering/leaving Iranian ports but will not impede transit to non-Iranian ports. Trump: blockade stays until US transaction with Iran complete. Iran threatens necessary measures if blockade not lifted. 49-nation Paris summit convened on Hormuz crisis.

watchpoints.event: US naval blockade implementation - does Navy actually intercept ships?
watchpoints.watch: Trump announced blockade via Truth Social Apr 12. Key: (1) Does Navy physically intercept commercial vessels? (2) Which ships - all, or just Iran-bound? (3) Does this apply to Chinese/Indian tankers that previously paid Iran toll? First enforcement action sets precedent. If US intercepts a Chinese vessel = major escalation with Beijing. IRGC vowed to retaliate against military vessels. Watch for first confrontation.

watchpoints.event: Mine-clearing progress - USS Peterson + Murphy + UUVs
watchpoints.watch: CENTCOM launched mine clearance Apr 11. UUV drones scanning for Maham 3 (moored) and Maham 7 (bottom) mines. Additional forces joining in coming days. UK/France minesweeping coalition forming. Iran threatened to target mine-clearing ships. Key: (1) How many mines found vs estimated? (2) Any new mines laid after clearing starts? (3) Timeline to certify safe commercial lanes - typically weeks to months. (4) IRGC attacks on clearing ships = massive escalation.

Reports of SEAL/Raider Quiet Entry on southern tip. Tripoli F-35Bs running SEAD. 50+ military targets struck Apr 7. IRGC claims missile hit on Tripoli (unconfirmed). Boxer ARG now in theater. 50,000 troops total. Is this active seizure, prep for seizure, or holding pattern during blockade? Watch: (1) Pentagon announces ground operation, (2) AIS data on Tripoli/Boxer positions, (3) Iran reports ground combat on Kharg.

watchpoints.event: Market reaction to blockade announcement (Monday open)
watchpoints.watch: Brent at 95.20 pre-announcement. Blockade announced Sunday - markets closed. Monday open will price in dual blockade + failed talks. Expect significant spike. If Brent breaks 120 = new regime. Oil market reaction signals how long traders expect closure to last.

watchpoints.event: Ceasefire status - does 2-week truce survive or formally collapse?
watchpoints.watch: Ceasefire agreed Apr 8 for 2 weeks (expires ~Apr 22). Talks collapsed Apr 11 but ceasefire not formally terminated. Trump announced blockade but not resumption of bombing. Iran has not declared ceasefire over. Ambiguous state: (1) If both sides resume strikes = ceasefire dead, war enters new phase, (2) If blockade coexists with ceasefire = strange limbo, pressure without kinetic escalation. Watch Iran next move.

watchpoints.event: Houthi Red Sea shipping decision - STILL the H4 swing factor
watchpoints.watch: Houthis hitting Israel but NOT Red Sea shipping. Strategic restraint so far. If Houthis activate Bab al-Mandab chokepoint = dual maritime crisis, Cape route overloaded, H4 surges to 0.45+. Operation Rough Rider (US strikes on Yemen) may have deterred maritime attacks. Watch: any Houthi statements about shipping, Red Sea incidents, or US force redeployment to Bab al-Mandab.

Resolution criterion (40 transits/day) now requires: (1) Iran stands down blockade, (2) US stands down counter-blockade, (3) Mines cleared from strait, (4) Insurance companies willing to cover transits, (5) 600+ stranded ships processed through. Each prerequisite adds weeks-months. Even optimistic scenario = months before normal traffic resumes. This is why H4 surged.

watchpoints.event: Islamabad channel - does Pakistan try round 2?
watchpoints.watch: 21hr talks failed but channel exists. Pakistan PM Sharif invested in mediator role. Face-to-face precedent established. After pressure from blockade, one or both sides may return. Watch: Pakistan diplomatic signals, back-channel reports, any new proposals. If no talks resume within 2 weeks = deadlock hardens.

watchpoints.event: Iran toll regime vs US interdiction - sovereignty clash
watchpoints.watch: Iran claims sovereignty right to toll ships. US says free passage non-negotiable. This was a collapse point in Islamabad talks. Now being tested physically - US intercepting toll-paying ships. If US successfully prevents all toll payments, Iran loses economic lifeline from blockade. If Iran retaliates against US enforcement = kinetic escalation at sea.

dependencies._docs: Cross-topic dependency declarations. Upstream topics whose posteriors this topic assumptions depend on.

upstream.reason: Houthi posture in the Red Sea/Bab al-Mandeb directly affects alternative shipping routes around the Horn of Africa. If Houthis de-escalate (H1), pressure to reopen Hormuz increases as the alternative route becomes viable. If Houthis maintain or escalate, the dual chokepoint closure extends Hormuz closure duration.
upstream.driftNote: Synced Apr 12. Prior assumptions stale: H1 was 0.32 (actual 0.15, drift 0.17), H3 was 0.18 (actual 0.30, drift 0.12). Both exceeded tolerance. Net effect: Houthis less likely to resume full attacks (H1 down), more likely to maintain de facto ceasefire on shipping (H3 up).
